page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 64 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier memory bandwidth, with read-only small block cached transfers reaching 7388.06 GB/sec and large block uncached transfers at 480.23 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations perform strongly, as evidenced by top-tier results in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, Geekbench multi-core scoring, and software compilation with Clang. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong categories respectively. Additionally, while text generation for medium and large LLM models ranks as strong, text generation for small 135M models is weak, representing a notable workload tradeoff.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high resource density and high memory throughput per unit of compute. This profile is effective for workloads that scale horizontally across many cores without requiring hyperthreading. The instance is appropriate for database operations, static web serving, and Redis deployments, where it achieves top-tier benchmark throughput. It is also suited for software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from high baseline network bandwidth and balanced memory-to-core ratios.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, and database operations, alongside strong software compilation and static web serving capabilities. However, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are relatively weaker. This resource configuration provides high resource density and memory throughput, making it effective for multi-threaded workloads, Redis deployments, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARMv9 Graviton-powered instance with 64 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARMv9 server with 64 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARMv9-based general-purpose instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and rapid multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Suited for Redis deployments, database operations, and static web serving
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
